rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CATHERINE E. MINASSIAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/09/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) Notice must be given to the limited conservator of the person and his attorney of record. CRC 7.51(c)(1) & (b). 2) The address of St. Luke's Hospital listed on the proof of service does not match the address set forth in paragraph 10 of the petition. 3) All reimbursements reported on Schedule C must identify original payees. LRSF 14.34(B). 4) The accounting does not show $14,851.33 due from the related trust (PTR-07-290409) as a receivable asset. 5) The ending carry value of 10 shares of VKBA bonds does not match the opening carry value of the same number of shares. 6) The filing fee for this accounting petition sought to be reimbursed to the petitioner's attorneys was $435. Note: A copy of a supplement or other paper must be served on the person who requested special notice. A proof of service must be filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AFANASIA KIBIL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/16/23. (No earlier available date.) No bank statements appear to have been filed. The executor's lengthy declaration in support of additional compensation for purported extraordinary services, in addition to not meeting the requirements of CRC 2.104, does not appear to fully meet the requirements of CRC 7.702. Many of the descriptions are also too brief to ascertain what was done, and none of the time entries appear to be for less than .3 hours. The executor repeatedly notes the length of time to administer the estate but does not explain why it required such a "dramatic duration of time" or more than four and a half years to close the estate. Attorney time spent to correct the Letters for the executor's misspelled name should not be the estate's burden. This Court also does not favor internal conferences but for the barest minimum for an attorney to review staff work. Deadline for delivering the proposed order and courtesy copies to the assigned examiner is 5/5/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GINETTE A. TERRELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/2/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Schedule C does not have sufficient (or any) descriptions for the disbursements [Probate Code Section 1062(b)]. None of the payments for caregiver expenses, for example, show the dates for such services and some appear to be quite high without any explanation. Living expenses and medical expenses (pages 27-29) also do not have sufficient (or any) descriptions. (2) Has the conservator purchased a pre-need funeral plan from SF Columbarium? (3) No Notice of Filing Inventory and Appraisal and How to Object appears to have been filed in connection with the Supplemental I&A filed on 11/2/22. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/21/23 Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The date of any order authorizing payment of fiduciary or attorney fees should be shown on the disbursement schedule, not just reported in the pleading. See JC form GC-400(C)(4).)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STUART SCOTT KOSITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. If the full statutory fee had been requested, detailed schedules would have been required. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUNG-DOW CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] The proposed order must show specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each heir, including the legal description of each real property not attached as an exhibit to the proposed order. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) A petition for approval of an account or a report accompanying the petition shall contain all the statements required by Probate Code Section 1064(a). Did the fiduciary have a family or affiliate relationship with any agent she hired during administration? (4) There are no disclosures regarding Probate Code Section 9202. (Notice to the Director of Health Care Services, to the Director of the California Victim Compensation and Government Claims Board if the personal representative or the attorney knows or has reason to believe that an heir or beneficiary is or has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation or confined in any county or city jail, etc., and to the Franchise Tax Board.) (5) This Court requires the specific date to be set forth when notice was given to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (6) There is no disclosure in the petition regarding the status of any tax returns filed or taxes due or paid. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Because the attorney fee requested is less than the statutory fee, the fee declaration will be disregarded. Please not, however, that a fee declaration must include a statement addressing a paralegal's qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450 if any portion of legal fees was billed by a paralegal.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LILLIAN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/13/23 to follow the outcome of the trust proceedings. Additionally, issue(s) with the new petition filed by Ray Torres on 3/1/23 (e-filed on 3/3/23) must also be addressed in time to provide a courtesy copy of any responsive pleading to the examiner before 6/2/23: (1) Item 2b of the petition is incomplete. (2) Item 2c of the petition is incomplete. (3) Item 3a of the petition is incomplete. (4) Item 3e(2) is not applicable, as there is no will. (5) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (6) The petitioner is missing from item 8. (Attorneys representing interested parties are not themselves interested parties who should be listed in item 8 of a Petition for Letters of Administration.) For both petitions, Notice of Petition to Administer Estate must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner before 6/2/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WARREN H. KING SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The temporary conservator of the estate of the decedent's surviving spouse filed a will contest and objections to the petition. The Court is to take a first look at the pleadings and determine how best to proceed. It should be noted that the letters of temporary conservatorship issued to the objector/contestant expire on 04/03/2023. It is unclear whether the temporary conservator has authority to prosecute the will contest, as no litigation authority is included in the temporary letters as an additional power that must be specifically authorized under Probate Code section 2252(c) & (d). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/16/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 3/8/23 is incompletely verified and still does not show that notice included the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) No corrected Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed yet with all of the information required in item 2. (3) Item 5a(1) or (2) of the petition should also be answered. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/5/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT G. PIETSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/16/23. (No earlier date available.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Now the petitioner discloses that the decedent also had a sister named Shirley. The sister was not listed in the petition. If she is deceased, her date of death must be set forth and the petitioner must address whether or not Shirley was survived by issue. (3) Other issues posted in the tentative ruling for the 2/7/23 hearing have not yet been addressed either: (a) The will names Carolyn Schreiber in Rocklin, CA, as a beneficiary. Carolyn "Shriver" in Roseville, CA, is listed in item 8 and received notice without any clarification about her identity; and (b) The will provision in paragraph 3 of the FIFTH section is a little unclear, but San Francisco State University is not listed in item 8 of the petition and did not get notice. The proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/5/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
